input.request_text_preview: Replicate the research paper 'All In One': The paper "All-in-one simulation-based inference" has been reproduced. This involves 5 major phases and 233 total subtasks. The workflow includes: Variance Exploding SDE (VESDE) is implemented, as described in A2.1; The Simformer model, training process and

method: LLM agent selection + greedy fleet assignment
llm_reasoning: This is a research replication workflow for 'All-in-One SBI' with clearly defined components (VESDE, Simformer, NPE/NRE/NLE baselines) already partially scoped into 5 phases. Given the moderate complexity (0.43) and low decomposability (0.30), the pipeline should be mostly sequential — literature extraction feeds experiment design, which feeds implementation, which feeds execution and analysis. Parallel execution is possible between the three baseline methods (NPE, NRE, NLE) and between Simformer training vs. baseline training, since they share no direct data dependencies once the experiment design is fixed.

llm_agent_details.name: literature_reviewer
llm_agent_details.prompt: You are replicating 'All-in-One Simulation-Based Inference'. Extract and document the following from the paper: (1) Full VESDE (Variance Exploding SDE) formulation from Appendix A2.1 — drift/diffusion coefficients, noise schedule σ(t), score function parameterization; (2) Simformer architecture — transformer backbone, conditioning mechanism, embedding of simulation parameters θ and data x; (3) Training objective — score matching loss, any regularization terms; (4) Inference procedure — reverse SDE/ODE solver, posterior sampling algorithm; (5) Baseline method specs for NPE (normalizing flows), NRE (ratio estimator network), and NLE (likelihood network); (6) Benchmark tasks used for evaluation and reported metrics (C2ST, MMD, or log-prob). Output a structured specification document with equations, hyperparameters, and architectural details for each component.
llm_agent_details.role: Extract and formalize all algorithmic specifications from the All-in-One SBI paper

llm_agent_details.name: experiment_designer
llm_agent_details.prompt: Using the extracted specifications from the literature reviewer, design a complete replication protocol for All-in-One SBI. Define: (1) The 5 replication phases with clear entry/exit criteria and subtask groupings (target ~233 subtasks total); (2) Benchmark tasks to run (e.g., two-moons, SLCP, Lotka-Volterra, or paper-specified tasks) with simulation budgets per task; (3) Shared data contracts — what each component (VESDE, Simformer, NPE, NRE, NLE) receives as input and produces as output; (4) Evaluation protocol — metrics, number of posterior samples, number of independent runs for statistical validity; (5) Hardware/compute assumptions (CPU vs GPU, estimated wall time per component); (6) Dependency graph showing which phases block which. Output a structured experiment plan in JSON-compatible format.
llm_agent_details.role: Design the full replication experimental protocol covering all 5 phases and benchmark tasks

llm_agent_details.name: code_generator
llm_agent_details.prompt: Implement the core All-in-One SBI system in Python (PyTorch). Required components: (1) VESDE implementation per Appendix A2.1 — implement σ(t) noise schedule, forward SDE, reverse SDE via Euler-Maruyama and probability flow ODE; (2) Score network — implement the Simformer transformer architecture with: positional embeddings for (θ, x) pairs, cross-attention conditioning, output head for ∇_θ log p(θ|x); (3) Training loop — score matching objective, gradient clipping, learning rate schedule, checkpoint saving; (4) Inference pipeline — posterior sampler using reverse SDE, batch inference for multiple observations. Use clean, modular code with type hints. Each component should be independently testable. Target compatibility: Python 3.10+, PyTorch 2.x, sbi library if applicable. Output complete, runnable source files.
llm_agent_details.role: Implement VESDE and the Simformer model with training and inference pipelines

llm_agent_details.name: code_gen
llm_agent_details.prompt: Implement the three SBI baseline methods for comparison against Simformer in the All-in-One SBI replication. Use a consistent interface across all three: (1) NPE (Neural Posterior Estimation) — implement using masked autoregressive flow or NSF as the density estimator, conditioned on observed data x; (2) NRE (Neural Ratio Estimation) — implement binary classifier to estimate log p(θ|x)/p(θ), train with contrastive pairs; (3) NLE (Neural Likelihood Estimation) — implement flow-based likelihood model p(x|θ), combine with prior for posterior via MCMC. All three should: accept the same simulator interface, expose a .train(θ_samples, x_samples) method and a .sample_posterior(x_obs, n_samples) method, log training metrics to the same schema. Leverage the `sbi` library where appropriate but implement custom variants if paper deviates. Output complete Python source with docstrings.
llm_agent_details.role: Implement the three baseline methods NPE, NRE, and NLE using consistent interfaces

llm_agent_details.name: tester
llm_agent_details.prompt: Write a comprehensive test suite for the All-in-One SBI replication codebase. Cover: (1) VESDE unit tests — verify σ(t) values at t=0 and t=T, check forward SDE produces increasing variance, validate reverse SDE converges toward prior; (2) Simformer tests — forward pass shape checks, gradient flow through score network, conditioning mechanism ablation (zero-out x embedding and verify score changes); (3) Training tests — loss decreases over 100 steps on a toy task (e.g., Gaussian likelihood), no NaN/Inf in gradients; (4) Inference tests — posterior samples from known Gaussian model should recover ground truth mean within 2σ; (5) Baseline parity tests — NPE, NRE, NLE all produce valid probability distributions (non-negative density, integrates to ~1 on grid); (6) Integration test — run full pipeline on two-moons task for 10 epochs and verify posterior samples are not degenerate. Use pytest with fixtures. Output complete test files.
llm_agent_details.role: Generate unit and integration tests for VESDE, Simformer, and all three baselines

llm_agent_details.name: experiment_runner
llm_agent_details.prompt: Run the full replication experiment suite for All-in-One SBI. Execute the following: (1) Run Simformer (VESDE + score network) on all benchmark tasks specified in the experiment plan — collect posterior samples, log training curves, record wall time; (2) Run NPE, NRE, NLE baselines on the same tasks with matched simulation budgets; (3) For each method × task combination: run N=10 independent trials with different random seeds, save posterior samples as .npy files, log per-trial metrics; (4) Collect all outputs into a structured results directory: results/{method}/{task}/trial_{seed}/; (5) Generate a raw results manifest (JSON) mapping each run to its output files and metadata (seed, n_simulations, runtime, final loss). Flag any runs that failed or produced degenerate posteriors. Output the results manifest and a brief run log.
llm_agent_details.role: Execute the replication experiments across all benchmark tasks for Simformer and baselines

llm_agent_details.name: result_analyzer
llm_agent_details.prompt: Analyze the experimental results from the All-in-One SBI replication. Perform: (1) Compute C2ST (Classifier Two-Sample Test) scores for each method × task — compare replicated posteriors against reference posteriors from the paper's reported values; (2) Compute MMD or log-prob metrics where reported in the paper; (3) Aggregate over N=10 trials: report mean ± std for each metric; (4) Build a comparison table: rows = benchmark tasks, columns = {Simformer (paper), Simformer (ours), NPE, NRE, NLE}, cells = primary metric with CI; (5) Identify statistically significant deviations from paper results (>2σ from reported); (6) Diagnose failure modes: did any method collapse, overfit, or underfit on specific tasks?; (7) Score overall replication fidelity as a percentage (metrics within 10% of reported = pass). Output a structured analysis report with tables and per-task commentary.
llm_agent_details.role: Compare Simformer vs baseline results, compute replication fidelity metrics

llm_agent_details.name: reporter
llm_agent_details.prompt: Produce the final replication report for 'All-in-One Simulation-Based Inference'. Structure the report as follows: (1) Executive Summary — was the replication successful? Overall fidelity score and key finding in 3 sentences; (2) Method Reproduction Summary — table of implemented components (VESDE, Simformer, NPE, NRE, NLE) with implementation status and any deviations from the paper; (3) Results Comparison — reproduce the paper's main results table with a side-by-side column for replicated results, highlighting matches and discrepancies; (4) Failure Analysis — document any components that could not be replicated exactly and hypothesize why (missing hyperparameters, architectural ambiguity, compute constraints); (5) Phase Completion Summary — map the 5 phases and 233 subtasks to completion status; (6) Recommendations — what would be needed to close remaining gaps. Format as a clean Markdown document suitable for inclusion in a research log or supplementary material.
llm_agent_details.role: Synthesize all phases into a final replication report with findings and fidelity assessment
